name: session-init
summary: Print session header with date, branch, last commit, uncommitted count. Warn if memory-sessions.md is bloated.
when_to_use: Any repo where you want a standard 'where am I' header at session start.
script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
printf 'Session start: %s\n' "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')" >&2
B=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo '(no-git)')
printf 'Branch: %s\n' "$B" >&2
L=$(git log --oneline -1 2>/dev/null || true)
[ -n "$L" ] && printf 'Last commit: %s\n' "$L" >&2
U=$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ' || echo 0)
printf 'Uncommitted: %s files\n' "$U" >&2
if [ -f .claude/rules/memory-sessions.md ]; then
  LC=$(wc -l < .claude/rules/memory-sessions.md)
  [ "$LC" -gt 100 ] && printf 'WARN: memory-sessions.md has %s lines — consider rotating\n' "$LC" >&2
fi
echo '{"decision":"approve"}'
verify: Start a new session; header appears in stderr output.
